title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
summary: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).

sections.overview: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To that end, the agency has added the vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-34197 (CVSS score: 8.8), to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian

Referenced vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-34197.
sections.impact:
Assessed severity: HIGH. CVSS score: 8.8/10.

Systems running affected software versions are at risk until patches or mitigations are applied.
sections.mitigation: Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls.
sections.context: Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

email_summary: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Tracked as CVE-2026-34197. Severity is rated high.
